free_text: Heavy fragment emission in alpha-induced reaction on
aluminum at 60 MeV
The target was made of aluminium foil of thickness
810 ug/cm2 having uniformity of better then 5%.
The different mass-fragments were
identified using a gas dE-silicon-E (300 um)
telescope.
To reject the energetic lighter(Z<=2) particles, a
silicon veto detector was used at the back of the E
detector.
The gas telescope was of axial configuration having an
active length of 4 cm and filled with a continuous
flow of P10 gas at 130 torr nominal pressure.
A thin polypropylene film of thickness 1.5-2.0 um was
used for the window of the gas detector.
The gas telescope subtended solid angle of 1.2 msr and
had an angular opening of degree.
Reetuparna Ghosh, Sylvia Badwar, B.M.Jyrwa
On. Finalized
